title: rubus macrophyllus subsp. orbifolius
intro: Rubus macrophyllus subsp. orbifolius, also known as the large-leaved bramble or Japanese bramble, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae), characterized by its broad, elliptical leaves and fragrant white flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in East Asian cultures, due to its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Rubus macrophyllus subsp. orbifolius are believed to include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against certain types of cancer. The plant is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
